root_text.an8.16:1.1: “Aṭṭhahi, bhikkhave, dhammehi samannāgato bhikkhu dūteyyaṁ gantumarahati.
root_text.an8.16:1.3: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu sotā ca hoti, sāvetā ca, uggahetā ca, dhāretā ca, viññātā ca, viññāpetā ca, kusalo ca sahitāsahitassa, no ca kalahakārako—
root_text.an8.16:1.4: imehi kho, bhikkhave, aṭṭhahi dhammehi samannāgato bhikkhu dūteyyaṁ gantumarahati.
root_text.an8.16:1.5: Aṭṭhahi, bhikkhave, dhammehi samannāgato sāriputto dūteyyaṁ gantumarahati.
root_text.an8.16:1.7: Idha, bhikkhave, sāriputto sotā ca hoti, sāvetā ca, uggahetā ca, dhāretā ca, viññātā ca, viññāpetā ca, kusalo ca sahitāsahitassa, no ca kalahakārako.
root_text.an8.16:1.8: Imehi kho, bhikkhave, aṭṭhahi dhammehi samannāgato sāriputto dūteyyaṁ gantumarahatīti.

translation_text.an8.16:1.1: “Mendicants, a mendicant with eight qualities is worthy of going on a mission.
translation_text.an8.16:1.3: It’s a mendicant who learns and educates others. They memorize and remember. They understand and help others understand. They’re skilled at knowing what’s on topic and what isn’t. And they don’t cause quarrels.
translation_text.an8.16:1.4: A mendicant with these eight qualities is worthy of going on a mission.
translation_text.an8.16:1.5: Having eight qualities Sāriputta is worthy of going on a mission.
translation_text.an8.16:1.7: He learns and educates others. He memorizes and remembers. He understands and helps others understand. He’s skilled at knowing what’s on topic and what isn’t. And he doesn’t cause quarrels.
translation_text.an8.16:1.8: Having these eight qualities Sāriputta is worthy of going on a mission.
translation_text.an8.16:2.2: at an assembly of fierce debaters.
translation_text.an8.16:2.3: They don’t miss out any words,
translation_text.an8.16:3.2: and they don’t tremble when questioned.
translation_text.an8.16:3.4: is worthy of going on a mission.”

variant_text.an8.16:2.1: na byathati → navedhati (bj); na byādhati (sya-all, pts1ed)
